[Fixing problems building 32 bit binaries](#fixing-problems-building-32-bit-binaries)\\n  - [Allocator](#allocator)\\n  - [Monotonic clock](#monotonic-clock)\\n  - [Verbose build](#verbose-build)\\n  - [Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:29:41.584248619Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

[Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:29:41.584248619Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}

[Build and run Redis with all data structures - Alpine 3.23+](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---alpine-323)\\n  - [Build and run Redis with all data structures - macOS 14 (Sonoma), 15 (Sequoia), 26 (Tahoe)](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---macos-14-sonoma-15-sequoia-26-tahoe)\\n  - [Fixing problems building 32 bit binaries](#fixing-problems-building-32-bit-binaries)\\n  - [Allocator](#allocator)\\n  - [Monotonic clock](#monotonic-clock)\\n  - [Verbose build](#verbose-build)\\n  - [Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:29:51.902322258Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

[Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:29:51.902322258Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}

Quick starts Client tools Client APIs Using commands Data types Compare data types Strings Arrays Geospatial Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets Redis for AI and search Programmability Use cases Pub/sub Reference Libraries and tools Redis products Commands Docs Docs → Develop with Redis → Redis data types Redis data types Overview of data types supported by Redis Redis is a data structure server. At its core, Redis provides a collection of native data types that help you solve a wide variety of problems, from caching to queuing to event processing . Below is a short description of each data type, with links to broader overviews and command references. Each overview includes a comprehensive tutorial with code samples. Data types Redis Open Source implements the following data types: Strings Bitmaps Bitfields Arrays Geospatial indexes Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic data types Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets See Compare data types for advice on which of the general-purpose data types is best for common tasks. Strings Redis strings are the most basic Redis data type, representing a sequence of bytes. For more information, see: Overview of Redis strings Redis string command reference Bitfields Redis bitfields efficiently encode multiple counters in a string value. Bitfields provide atomic get, set, and increment operations and support different overflow policies. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitfields The BITFIELD command. Bitmaps Redis bitmaps let you perform bitwise operations on strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitmaps Redis bitmap command reference Arrays Redis arrays are sparse, index-addressable sequences of strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis arrays Redis array command reference Geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes are useful for finding locations within a given geographic radius or bounding box. For more information, see: Overview of Redis geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes command reference Hashes Redis hashes are record types modeled as collections of field-value pairs. As such, Redis hashes resemble Python dictionaries , Java HashMaps , and Ruby hashes . For more information, see: Overview of Redis hashes Redis hashes command reference JSON Redis JSON provides structured, hierarchical arrays and key-value objects that match the popular JSON text file format. You can import JSON text into Redis objects and access, modify, and query individual data elements. For more information, see: Overview of Redis JSON JSON command reference Lists Redis lists are lists of strings sorted by insertion order. For more information, see: Overview of Redis lists Redis list command reference Probabilistic data types These data types let you gather and calculate statistics in a way that is approximate but highly efficient. The following types are available: Bloom filter Count-min sketch Cuckoo filter HyperLogLog t-digest Top-K Bloom filter Redis Bloom filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Bloom filters Bloom filter command reference Count-min sketch Redis Count-min sketch estimate the frequency of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Count-min sketch overview Count-min sketch command reference Cuckoo filter Redis Cuckoo filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. They are similar to Bloom filters but with slightly different trade-offs between features and performance. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Cuckoo filters Cuckoo filter command reference HyperLogLog The Redis HyperLogLog data structures provide probabilistic estimates of the cardinality (i.e., number of elements) of large sets. For more information, see: Overview of Redis HyperLogLog Redis HyperLogLog command reference t-digest Redis t-digest structures estimate percentiles from a stream of data values. For more information, see: Redis t-digest overview t-digest command reference Top-K Redis Top-K structures estimate the ranking of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Top-K overview Top-K command reference Sets Redis sets are unordered collections of unique strings that act like the sets from your favorite programming language (for example, Java HashSets , Python sets , and so on). With a Redis set, you can add, remove, and test for existence in O(1) time (in other words, regardless of the number of set elements). For more information, see: Overview of Redis sets Redis set command reference Sorted sets Redis sorted sets are collections of unique strings that maintain order by each string's associated score. For more information, see: Overview of Redis sorted sets Redis sorted set command reference Streams A Redis stream is a data structure that acts like an append-only log. Streams help record events in the order they occur and then syndicate them for processing. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Streams Redis Streams command reference Time series Redis time series structures let you store and query timestamped data points. For more information, see: Redis time series overview Time series command reference Vector sets Redis vector sets are a specialized data type designed for managing high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and efficient vector similarity search within Redis. Vector sets are optimized for use cases involving machine learning, recommendation systems, and semantic search, where each vector represents a data point in multi-dimensional space. Vector sets supports the HNSW (hierarchical navigable small world) algorithm, allowing you to store, index, and query vectors based on the cosine similarity metric. With vector sets, Redis provides native support for hybrid search, combining vector similarity with structured filters . For more information, see: Overview of Redis vector sets Redis vector set command reference Adding extensions To extend the features provided by the included data types, use one of these options: Write your own custom server-side functions in Lua . Write your own Redis module using the modules API or check out the community-supported modules . event:tool_call_finished
data:{"runId":"run_322910085246746624","stepId":"call_74593fdc58604769b07fb823","toolInvocationId":"call_74593fdc58604769b07fb823","toolCallId":"call_74593fdc58604769b07fb823","toolId":"web_fetch","riskLevel":"HIGH","summary":"{\"url\":\"https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/\",\"status\":\"FETCHED\",\"title\":\"Redis data types | Docs {\\\"categories\\\":[\\\"docs\\\",\\\"develop\\\",\\\"stack\\\",\\\"oss\\\",\\\"rs\\\",\\\"rc\\\",\\\"oss\\\",\\\"kubernetes\\\",\\\"clients\\\"],\\\"descripti\",\"mimeType\":\"text/html\",\"reasonCode\":null,\"truncated\":false,\"contentWarning\":\"UNTRUSTED_EXTERNAL_CONTENT\",\"contentText\":\"Redis data types | Docs {\\\"categories\\\":[\\\"docs\\\",\\\"develop\\\",\\\"stack\\\",\\\"oss\\\",\\\"rs\\\",\\\"rc\\\",\\\"oss\\\",\\\"kubernetes\\\",\\\"clients\\\"],\\\"description\\\":\\\"Overview of data types supported by Redis\\\",\\\"duplicateOf\\\":\\\"head:data-ai-metadata\\\",\\\"location\\\":\\\"body\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Redis data types\\\",\\\"tableOfContents\\\":{\\\"sections\\\":[]},\\\"codeExamples\\\":[]} AI agent builder Redis install Data types Tools Community Docs Search Login Sign up Open search Open main menu AI agent builder Redis install Data types Tools Community Docs Sign up Login Develop with Redis What&#39;s new? Quick starts Client tools Client APIs Using commands Data types Compare data types Strings Arrays Geospatial Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets Redis for AI and search Programmability Use cases Pub/sub Reference Libraries and tools Redis products Commands Docs Docs → Develop with Redis → Redis data types Redis data types Overview of data types supported by Redis Redis is a data structure server. At its core, Redis provides a collection of native data types that help you solve a wide variety of problems, from caching to queuing to event processing . Below is a short description of each data type, with links to broader overviews and command references. Each overview includes a comprehensive tutorial with code samples. Data types Redis Open Source implements the following data types: Strings Bitmaps Bitfields Arrays Geospatial indexes Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic data types Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets See Compare data types for advice on which of the general-purpose data types is best for common tasks. Strings Redis strings are the most basic Redis data type, representing a sequence of bytes. For more information, see: Overview of Redis strings Redis string command reference Bitfields Redis bitfields efficiently encode multiple counters in a string value. Bitfields provide atomic get, set, and increment operations and support different overflow policies. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitfields The BITFIELD command. Bitmaps Redis bitmaps let you perform bitwise operations on strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitmaps Redis bitmap command reference Arrays Redis arrays are sparse, index-addressable sequences of strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis arrays Redis array command reference Geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes are useful for finding locations within a given geographic radius or bounding box. For more information, see: Overview of Redis geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes command reference Hashes Redis hashes are record types modeled as collections of field-value pairs. As such, Redis hashes resemble Python dictionaries , Java HashMaps , and Ruby hashes . For more information, see: Overview of Redis hashes Redis hashes command reference JSON Redis JSON provides structured, hierarchical arrays and key-value objects that match the popular JSON text file format. You can import JSON text into Redis objects and access, modify, and query individual data elements. For more information, see: Overview of Redis JSON JSON command reference Lists Redis lists are lists of strings sorted by insertion order. For more information, see: Overview of Redis lists Redis list command reference Probabilistic data types These data types let you gather and calculate statistics in a way that is approximate but highly efficient. The following types are available: Bloom filter Count-min sketch Cuckoo filter HyperLogLog t-digest Top-K Bloom filter Redis Bloom filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Bloom filters Bloom filter command reference Count-min sketch Redis Count-min sketch estimate the frequency of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Count-min sketch overview Count-min sketch command reference Cuckoo filter Redis Cuckoo filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. They are similar to Bloom filters but with slightly different trade-offs between features and performance. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Cuckoo filters Cuckoo filter command reference HyperLogLog The Redis HyperLogLog data structures provide probabilistic estimates of the cardinality (i.e., number of elements) of large sets. For more information, see: Overview of Redis HyperLogLog Redis HyperLogLog command reference t-digest Redis t-digest structures estimate percentiles from a stream of data values. For more information, see: Redis t-digest overview t-digest command reference Top-K Redis Top-K structures estimate the ranking of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Top-K overview Top-K command reference Sets Redis sets are unordered collections of unique strings that act like the sets from your favorite programming language (for example, Java HashSets , Python sets , and so on). With a Redis set, you can add, remove, and test for existence in O(1) time (in other words, regardless of the number of set elements). For more information, see: Overview of Redis sets Redis set command reference Sorted sets Redis sorted sets are collections of unique strings that maintain order by each string's associated score. For more information, see: Overview of Redis sorted sets Redis sorted set command reference Streams A Redis stream is a data structure that acts like an append-only log. Streams help record events in the order they occur and then syndicate them for processing. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Streams Redis Streams command reference Time series Redis time series structures let you store and query timestamped data points. For more information, see: Redis time series overview Time series command reference Vector sets Redis vector sets are a specialized data type designed for managing high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and efficient vector similarity search within Redis. Vector sets are optimized for use cases involving machine learning, recommendation systems, and semantic search, where each vector represents a data point in multi-dimensional space. Vector sets supports the HNSW (hierarchical navigable small world) algorithm, allowing you to store, index, and query vectors based on the cosine similarity metric. With vector sets, Redis provides native support for hybrid search, combining vector similarity with structured filters . For more information, see: Overview of Redis vector sets Redis vector set command reference Adding extensions To extend the features provided by the included data types, use one of these options: Write your own custom server-side functions in Lua . Write your own Redis module using the modules API or check out the community-supported modules . Redis 开源内存数据存储引擎：架构、特性与应用场景深度解析\\n\\n## 核心定位\\nRedis 是目前首选的、速度最快且功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档及向量查询引擎。它不仅仅是一个简单的键值存储，而是一个多功能的数据平台，旨在满足现代应用对低延迟和高吞吐量的严苛要求。\\n\\n## 关键特性与架构优势\\n\\n*   **极致性能**：基于内存运行，提供亚毫秒级（sub-millisecond）延迟，确保极高的响应速度。\\n*   **持久化机制**：支持 RDB（快照）和 AOF（追加日志）两种持久化策略，平衡数据安全性与性能。\\n*   **高可用与扩展性**：\\n    *   **Redis Sentinel**：提供高可用性监控和故障转移。\\n    *   **Redis Cluster**：支持数据分片（Sharding）和高可用，实现水平扩展。\\n*   **高级功能支持**：\\n    *   **Lua 脚本**：支持原子性操作，简化复杂逻辑处理。\\n    *   **Modules API**：允许通过扩展模块增强核心功能。\\n    *   **RESP 协议**：高效且易于实现的客户端-服务器通信协议。\\n\\n## 丰富的数据结构\\nRedis 提供了多样化的原生数据结构，以适配不同的业务场景：\\n\\n*   **基础类型**：Strings（字符串）、Hashes（哈希）、Lists（列表）、Sets（集合）、Sorted Sets（有序集合）。\\n*   **高级类型**：\\n    *   **JSON**：原生支持 JSON 文档存储与查询。\\n    *   **Geospatial**：地理空间索引，用于位置服务。\\n    *   **Bitmaps & Bitfields**：位图与位字段，用于高效的状态标记和计数。\\n    *   **Streams**：流式数据结构，支持消息队列功能。\\n    *   **Time Series**：时间序列数据，适用于物联网和监控场景。\\n    *   **Vector Sets**：向量集合，专为生成式 AI（GenAI）和语义搜索设计。\\n*   **概率型数据结构**：\\n    *   Bloom Filter（布隆过滤器）、Count-min Sketch、Cuckoo Filter、HyperLogLog、t-digest、Top-K。\\n    *   *优势*：在节省内存的同时，提供近似计数、去重和排名功能。\\n\\n## 主要应用场景\\n\\n1.  **缓存（Caching）**：作为高性能缓存层，减轻后端数据库压力。\\n2.  **分布式会话存储（Distributed Session Store）**：在多节点应用中共享用户会话状态。\\n3.  **NoSQL 数据存储**：作为轻量级、高性能的 NoSQL 数据库使用。\\n4.  **数据结构服务器**：利用其丰富的数据结构处理复杂逻辑。\\n5.  **搜索与查询引擎（Redis Search）**：提供全文搜索和过滤能力。\\n6.  **事件存储与消息代理（Event Store & Message Broker）**：利用 Streams 实现实时消息传递。\\n7.  **生成式 AI 向量存储（Vector Store for GenAI）**：存储和检索嵌入向量，支持语义搜索和推荐系统。\\n8.  **实时分析（Real-Time Analytics）**：处理高频实时数据流，进行即时统计和分析。\\n\\n## 总结\\nRedis 凭借其内存级性能、丰富的数据结构、强大的持久化与集群能力，已成为现代软件架构中不可或缺的基础组件。无论是构建高性能缓存、实时消息系统，还是支持前沿的 AI 应用，Redis 都能提供稳定且高效的解决方案。\"}","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:30:47.898421942Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

Sets**：向量集合，专为生成式 AI（GenAI）和语义搜索设计。\\n*   **概率型数据结构**：\\n    *   Bloom Filter（布隆过滤器）、Count-min Sketch、Cuckoo Filter、HyperLogLog、t-digest、Top-K。\\n    *   *优势*：在节省内存的同时，提供近似计数、去重和排名功能。\\n\\n## Today we will explore Redis, the industry-standard in-memory data store. We will cover its core architecture, key features like persistence and clustering, and its diverse application scenarios ranging from traditional caching to modern GenAI vector storage. This overview is designed for technical decision-makers and architects to evaluate Redis for their infrastructure.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA sleek, modern 3D render of a glowing blue cube representing memory, with data streams flowing into it, set against a dark tech background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 2: What is Redis?\\n\\n**Title:** Redis: More Than Just a Cache\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Definition:** An open-source, in-memory data structure store and server.\\n*   **Core Identity:** Preferred cache, data structure server, and document/vector query engine.\\n*   **Performance:** Delivers sub-millisecond latency due to in-memory processing.\\n*   **Versatility:** Supports complex data structures, not just simple key-value pairs.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is often mistaken for just a caching layer, but it is a full-featured data structure server. Its primary advantage is speed: because data resides in RAM, it offers sub-millisecond latency. It supports a wide variety of data structures, making it suitable for use cases far beyond simple session storage, including NoSQL databases, search engines, and message brokers.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nAn infographic showing a central hub labeled \\\"Redis\\\" connecting to various icons representing databases, caches, and AI models, emphasizing its central role in modern tech stacks.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 3: Core Architecture & Performance\\n\\n**Title:** In-Memory Architecture & Latency\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **In-Memory Storage:** All data is stored in RAM for maximum speed.\\n*   **Sub-Millisecond Latency:** Ideal for high-throughput, low-latency requirements.\\n*   **Single-Threaded Event Loop:** Simplifies concurrency and ensures atomic operations.\\n*   **RESP Protocol:** Efficient binary-safe protocol for client-server communication.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe foundation of Redis's performance is its in-memory architecture. By keeping data in RAM, it eliminates the I/O bottlenecks associated with disk-based databases. It uses a single-threaded event loop model, which simplifies concurrency management and ensures that operations are atomic. The RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol) is optimized for efficient binary-safe communication between clients and the server.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA diagram comparing disk-based storage (slow, layered) vs. in-memory storage (fast, direct access), highlighting the speed difference with a lightning bolt icon.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 4: Rich Data Structures\\n\\n**Title:** Beyond Key-Value: Advanced Data Types\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Basic Types:** Strings, Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, Hashes.\\n*   **Advanced Types:** JSON, Geospatial indexes, Bitmaps, Bitfields.\\n*   **Probabilistic Data Structures:** Bloom filters, HyperLogLog, Count-min sketch, Cuckoo filters.\\n*   **Specialized Types:** Streams (for event sourcing), Time series, Vector sets.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis supports a rich set of data structures that allow for complex data modeling. Beyond standard strings and hashes, it offers JSON support for document storage, geospatial indexing for location-based services, and probabilistic structures like Bloom filters for efficient membership testing. It also includes specialized types like Streams for event sourcing and Vector sets for AI applications.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA visual grid displaying icons for different data structures: a string, a hash map, a JSON object, a map pin for geospatial, and a neural network node for vectors.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 5: Persistence & Durability\\n\\n**Title:** Ensuring Data Durability\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **RDB (Redis Database):** Point-in-time snapshots for efficient backup and recovery.\\n*   **AOF (Append Only File):** Logs every write operation for fine-grained durability.\\n*   **Configurable Policies:** Choose between speed (RDB) and durability (AOF) or both.\\n*   **Hybrid Persistence:** Redis 4.0+ supports combining RDB and AOF for best of both worlds.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nWhile Redis is in-memory, it provides robust persistence options to prevent data loss. RDB creates periodic snapshots, which are fast and compact, ideal for backups. AOF logs every write operation, offering higher durability at the cost of larger file sizes. Modern Redis instances can use both, leveraging RDB for fast startup and AOF for minimal data loss.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA timeline graphic showing periodic snapshots (RDB) and a continuous log file (AOF) being written to disk, illustrating the persistence mechanisms.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 6: High Availability & Scalability\\n\\n**Title:** Enterprise-Grade Reliability\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Master-Slave Replication:** Asynchronous replication for read scaling and backup.\\n*   **Redis Sentinel:** Automated failover and monitoring for high availability.\\n*   **Redis Cluster:** Native sharding for horizontal scaling and fault tolerance.\\n*   **Automatic Partitioning:** Data is distributed across multiple nodes seamlessly.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nFor production environments, Redis offers built-in high availability and scalability. Master-Slave replication allows for read scaling and data redundancy. Redis Sentinel monitors the cluster and automatically promotes a slave to master if the primary fails. For massive scale, Redis Cluster provides automatic sharding, distributing data across multiple nodes to handle large datasets and high throughput.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA network diagram showing a central master node connected to multiple slave nodes, with a Sentinel node monitoring them, and a separate cluster view showing data sharded across multiple nodes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 7: Key Use Cases: Caching & Sessions\\n\\n**Title:** Primary Use Cases: Caching & Session Management\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Application Caching:** Reduce database load and improve response times.\\n*   **Distributed Session Store:** Share user sessions across multiple application instances.\\n*   **Rate Limiting:** Use atomic counters and expiration to control API usage.\\n*   **Real-Time Analytics:** Aggregate metrics and counters with low overhead.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe most common use case for Redis is caching, which significantly reduces latency and database load. It is also the de facto standard for distributed session storage, allowing web applications to scale horizontally. Additionally, its atomic operations make it ideal for rate limiting APIs and performing real-time analytics by aggregating counters and metrics.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA web application architecture diagram showing multiple web servers connecting to a central Redis cache, with a database in the background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 8: Key Use Cases: Search & Messaging\\n\\n**Title:** Advanced Use Cases: Search & Messaging\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Redis Search:** Full-text search engine with faceting and filtering.\\n*   **Event Store & Message Broker:** Redis Streams for reliable message queuing.\\n*   **Pub/Sub:** Real-time messaging for chat, notifications, and live updates.\\n*   **NoSQL Data Store:** Storing complex documents (JSON) directly.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis extends beyond simple storage. With Redis Search, it provides full-text search capabilities, including faceting and filtering, without needing a separate search engine. Redis Streams act as a reliable message broker for event sourcing and microservices communication. Its Pub/Sub system enables real-time messaging for applications like chat or live notifications.\\n\\n*...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:32:46.718398112Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

Today we will explore Redis, the industry-standard in-memory data store. We will cover its core architecture, key features like persistence and clustering, and its diverse application scenarios ranging from traditional caching to modern GenAI vector storage. This overview is designed for technical decision-makers and architects to evaluate Redis for their infrastructure.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA sleek, modern 3D render of a glowing blue cube representing memory, with data streams flowing into it, set against a dark tech background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 2: What is Redis?\\n\\n**Title:** Redis: More Than Just a Cache\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Definition:** An open-source, in-memory data structure store and server.\\n*   **Core Identity:** Preferred cache, data structure server, and document/vector query engine.\\n*   **Performance:** Delivers sub-millisecond latency due to in-memory processing.\\n*   **Versatility:** Supports complex data structures, not just simple key-value pairs.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is often mistaken for just a caching layer, but it is a full-featured data structure server. Its primary advantage is speed: because data resides in RAM, it offers sub-millisecond latency. It supports a wide variety of data structures, making it suitable for use cases far beyond simple session storage, including NoSQL databases, search engines, and message brokers.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nAn infographic showing a central hub labeled \\\"Redis\\\" connecting to various icons representing databases, caches, and AI models, emphasizing its central role in modern tech stacks.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 3: Core Architecture & Performance\\n\\n**Title:** In-Memory Architecture & Latency\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **In-Memory Storage:** All data is stored in RAM for maximum speed.\\n*   **Sub-Millisecond Latency:** Ideal for high-throughput, low-latency requirements.\\n*   **Single-Threaded Event Loop:** Simplifies concurrency and ensures atomic operations.\\n*   **RESP Protocol:** Efficient binary-safe protocol for client-server communication.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe foundation of Redis's performance is its in-memory architecture. By keeping data in RAM, it eliminates the I/O bottlenecks associated with disk-based databases. It uses a single-threaded event loop model, which simplifies concurrency management and ensures that operations are atomic. The RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol) is optimized for efficient binary-safe communication between clients and the server.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA diagram comparing disk-based storage (slow, layered) vs. in-memory storage (fast, direct access), highlighting the speed difference with a lightning bolt icon.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 4: Rich Data Structures\\n\\n**Title:** Beyond Key-Value: Advanced Data Types\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Basic Types:** Strings, Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, Hashes.\\n*   **Advanced Types:** JSON, Geospatial indexes, Bitmaps, Bitfields.\\n*   **Probabilistic Data Structures:** Bloom filters, HyperLogLog, Count-min sketch, Cuckoo filters.\\n*   **Specialized Types:** Streams (for event sourcing), Time series, Vector sets.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis supports a rich set of data structures that allow for complex data modeling. Beyond standard strings and hashes, it offers JSON support for document storage, geospatial indexing for location-based services, and probabilistic structures like Bloom filters for efficient membership testing. It also includes specialized types like Streams for event sourcing and Vector sets for AI applications.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA visual grid displaying icons for different data structures: a string, a hash map, a JSON object, a map pin for geospatial, and a neural network node for vectors.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 5: Persistence & Durability\\n\\n**Title:** Ensuring Data Durability\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **RDB (Redis Database):** Point-in-time snapshots for efficient backup and recovery.\\n*   **AOF (Append Only File):** Logs every write operation for fine-grained durability.\\n*   **Configurable Policies:** Choose between speed (RDB) and durability (AOF) or both.\\n*   **Hybrid Persistence:** Redis 4.0+ supports combining RDB and AOF for best of both worlds.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nWhile Redis is in-memory, it provides robust persistence options to prevent data loss. RDB creates periodic snapshots, which are fast and compact, ideal for backups. AOF logs every write operation, offering higher durability at the cost of larger file sizes. Modern Redis instances can use both, leveraging RDB for fast startup and AOF for minimal data loss.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA timeline graphic showing periodic snapshots (RDB) and a continuous log file (AOF) being written to disk, illustrating the persistence mechanisms.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 6: High Availability & Scalability\\n\\n**Title:** Enterprise-Grade Reliability\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Master-Slave Replication:** Asynchronous replication for read scaling and backup.\\n*   **Redis Sentinel:** Automated failover and monitoring for high availability.\\n*   **Redis Cluster:** Native sharding for horizontal scaling and fault tolerance.\\n*   **Automatic Partitioning:** Data is distributed across multiple nodes seamlessly.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nFor production environments, Redis offers built-in high availability and scalability. Master-Slave replication allows for read scaling and data redundancy. Redis Sentinel monitors the cluster and automatically promotes a slave to master if the primary fails. For massive scale, Redis Cluster provides automatic sharding, distributing data across multiple nodes to handle large datasets and high throughput.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA network diagram showing a central master node connected to multiple slave nodes, with a Sentinel node monitoring them, and a separate cluster view showing data sharded across multiple nodes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 7: Key Use Cases: Caching & Sessions\\n\\n**Title:** Primary Use Cases: Caching & Session Management\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Application Caching:** Reduce database load and improve response times.\\n*   **Distributed Session Store:** Share user sessions across multiple application instances.\\n*   **Rate Limiting:** Use atomic counters and expiration to control API usage.\\n*   **Real-Time Analytics:** Aggregate metrics and counters with low overhead.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe most common use case for Redis is caching, which significantly reduces latency and database load. It is also the de facto standard for distributed session storage, allowing web applications to scale horizontally. Additionally, its atomic operations make it ideal for rate limiting APIs and performing real-time analytics by aggregating counters and metrics.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA web application architecture diagram showing multiple web servers connecting to a central Redis cache, with a database in the background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 8: Key Use Cases: Search & Messaging\\n\\n**Title:** Advanced Use Cases: Search & Messaging\\n\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Redis Search:** Full-text search engine with faceting and filtering.\\n*   **Event Store & Message Broker:** Redis Streams for reliable message queuing.\\n*   **Pub/Sub:** Real-time messaging for chat, notifications, and live updates.\\n*   **NoSQL Data Store:** Storing complex documents (JSON) directly.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis extends beyond simple storage. With Redis Search, it provides full-text search capabilities, including faceting and filtering, without needing a separate search engine. Redis Streams act as a reliable message broker for event sourcing and microservices communication. Its Pub/Sub system enables real-time messaging for applications like chat or live notifications.\\n\\n*...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:32:46.718398112Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}

data:{"eventId":"322911778571157504","eventSeq":"3611","eventType":"STEP_FINISHED","runId":"run_322910085246746624","stepId":null,"timestamp":"2026-06-10T01:36:07.422770534Z","typedPayload":{"runId":"run_322910085246746624","stepId":"model-turn-15","stepNo":15,"stepType":"MODEL_TURN","status":"SUCCEEDED","title":"Model turn","summary":null,"startedAt":"2026-06-10T01:34:27.942649144Z","finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:36:07.420855553Z","durationMs":"99478","errorCode":null,"message":"已达到工具调用限制。现在基于已有产物输出最终 Markdown。# Redis开源内存数据存储引擎：项目图文介绍\n\n## 一、项目概览\n\n**Redis**（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的**内存数据结构存储系统**，被广泛用作数据库、缓存和消息代理。它支持丰富的数据结构，包括字符串（Strings）、哈希（Hashes）、列表（Lists）、集合（Sets）、有序集合（Sorted Sets）、位图（Bitmaps）、地理空间索引（Geospatial）、JSON文档、流（Streams）、时间序列（Time Series）以及向量集合（Vector Sets）等。Redis的核心定位是：\n- **首选的、速度最快且功能最丰富的缓存服务器**\n- **数据结构服务器**：提供底层数据结构和高级语义（计数器、队列、排行榜、限流器等）\n- **文档和向量查询引擎**：通过 Redis Search支持全文搜索、向量搜索、地理空间查询\n- **事件存储和消息代理**：支持 Streams、Pub/Sub、优先级队列等Redis由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护，采用 BSD开源许可证。\n\n## 二、架构设计\n\n### 2.1整体架构分层Redis的架构采用分层设计，从网络通信到数据存储逐层抽象：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"]\nNet[\"网络层 (Networking)\"]\nRESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"]\nCmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"]\nData[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"]\nMem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"]\nPers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"]\nClient --> Net\nNet --> RESP\nRESP --> Cmd\nCmd --> Data\nData --> Mem\nMem --> Pers\nclassDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;\nclassDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b;\nclassDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100;\nclassDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20;\nclass Client client;\nclass Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core;\nclass Mem,Pers storage;\n```\n\n**各层职责说明：**|层级 |职责 |\n|------|------|\n| **网络层** |处理 TCP连接、多路复用（epoll/kqueue）、TLS加密通信 |\n| **协议层** |实现 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol），高效解析客户端命令 |\n| **命令层** |命令路由、权限检查、事务处理、Lua脚本执行 |\n| **数据模型层** |实现各种数据结构（String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set、JSON、Stream等） |\n| **内存存储层** |管理内存分配、键空间、过期键淘汰策略 |\n| **持久化层** | RDB快照和 AOF追加日志，确保数据不丢失 |\n\n### 2.2高可用架构Redis提供两种高可用方案：\n- **Redis Sentinel**：监控主从节点，自动故障转移，保证服务可用性\n- **Redis Cluster**：原生分片支持，自动数据分区，实现水平扩展\n\n## 三、架构图\n\n以下是 Redis整体架构的分层示意图：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"]\nNet[\"网络层 (Networking)\"]\nRESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"]\nCmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"]\nData[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"]\nMem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"]\nPers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"]\nClient --> Net\nNet --> RESP\nRESP --> Cmd\nCmd --> Data\nData --> Mem\nMem --> Pers\nclassDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;\nclassDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b;\nclassDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100;\nclassDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20;\nclass Client client;\nclass Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core;\nclass Mem,Pers storage;\n```\n\n## 四、流程图\n\n### 4.1用户请求处理流程\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\nparticipant Client\nparticipant RedisServer as Redis Server\nparticipant NetworkLayer as Network Layer\nparticipant RESPParser as RESP Parser\nparticipant CmdProcessor as Command Processor\nparticipant DataStructures as Data Structures\nparticipant MemoryStore as Memory Store\nparticipant Persistence as Persistence Layer\nClient->>RedisServer: SEND command (RESP)\nRedisServer->>NetworkLayer:接收 TCP连接\nNetworkLayer->>RESPParser:解析协议\nRESPParser->>CmdProcessor:执行命令\nCmdProcessor->>DataStructures:读写数据\nDataStructures->>MemoryStore:内存操作\nMemoryStore->>Persistence: RDB/AOF写入\nMemoryStore->>Client:返回结果\n```\n\n### 4.2 Agent执行流程\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nA[开始] --> B[读取 GitHub仓库]\nB --> C[读取文档/源码]\nC --> D[web_fetch补充信息]\nD --> E[生成架构图/流程图]\nE --> F[生成项目介绍图片]\nF --> G[生成中文长文稿件]\nG --> H[生成演示文稿结构]\nH --> I[生成 Web版式草案]\nI --> J[输出最终 Markdown]\n```\n\n## 五、核心逻辑\n\n### 5.1单线程事件循环\n\n模型\n\nRedis 采用单线程事件循环模型处理客户端请求，这带来了以下优势：\n- **原子性操作**：所有命令按顺序执行，无需锁机制\n- **低延迟**：避免上下文切换和锁竞争\n- **简化并发模型**：开发者无需担心竞态条件\n\n### 5.2持久化机制Redis提供两种持久化策略：\n\n|策略 |说明 |优点 |缺点 |\n|------|------|------|------|\n| **RDB** |定期创建数据快照 |文件紧凑、恢复速度快 |可能丢失最后一次快照后的数据 |\n| **AOF** |记录每条写操作日志 |数据安全性高 |文件体积较大、恢复速度较慢 |Redis4.0+支持混合持久化，结合 RDB和 AOF的优点。\n\n### 5.3复制与分片\n- **主从复制**：异步复制，从节点可用于读扩展和故障转移\n- **Redis Cluster**：自动数据分片（16384个哈希槽），支持节点故障自动转移\n\n## 六、重点特性\n\n### 6.1丰富的数据结构\n\nRedis支持15+种原生数据结构：|类型 |说明 |适用场景 |\n|------|------|----------|\n| **Strings** |二进制安全的字符串 |缓存、计数器、会话存储 |\n| **Hashes** |字段-值对集合 |用户信息存储 |\n| **Lists** |双向链表 |消息队列、时间线 |\n| **Sets** |无序唯一集合 |去重、共同好友 |\n| **Sorted Sets** |带分数的有序集合 |排行榜、延迟队列 |\n| **JSON** |原生 JSON支持 |文档存储 |\n| **Streams** |追加日志式数据结构 |事件溯源、消息队列 |\n| **Vector Sets** |高维向量集合 | AI向量搜索、语义检索 |\n| **Bitmaps** |位数组 |用户活跃状态统计 |\n| **Geospatial** |地理空间索引 |位置服务、附近的人 |\n| **HyperLogLog** |概率基数统计 |独立访客计数 |\n| **Bloom Filter** |布隆过滤器 |缓存穿透防护 |\n\n### 6.2关键优势\n- **极致性能**：亚毫秒级延迟，10万+ QPS\n- **灵活性**：支持多种数据结构和高级语义\n- **可扩展性**：Modules API允许扩展新命令\n- **简洁性**：RESP协议简单高效\n- **普及性**：被全球数百万应用广泛使用\n\n## 七、关键文件证据表\n\n|文件路径 |用途 |证据说明 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| `README.md` |项目概述、核心特性、使用场景 |确认 Redis的定位、数据结构、持久化机制、高可用方案 |\n| `CONTRIBUTING.md` |代码贡献指南 |确认项目开源社区规范 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/` |开发文档 |确认客户端 API、数据操作方式 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/` |数据类型文档 |确认15+种数据结构的详细说明 |\n\n## 八、生成图片引用\n\n以下是生成的项目介绍视觉图：![Redis架构介绍图](https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/96b561e966ea48619ad7575fb67c9464.png)>图片描述：Redis内存数据存储引擎的架构可视化，展示核心内存存储、数据结构、持久化层和客户端连接。\n\n## 九、生成稿件和版式产物摘要\n\n### 长文稿件摘要newsletter_generation生成的中文长文《Redis开源内存数据存储引擎：架构、特性与应用场景深度解析》包含以下核心内容：\n- **核心定位**：Redis是首选的、速度最快且功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档及向量查询引擎\n- **关键特性**：极致性能（亚毫秒级延迟）、持久化机制（RDB/AOF）、高可用与扩展性（Sentinel/Cluster）、高级功能（Lua脚本、Modules API、RESP协议）\n- **丰富的数据结构**：基础类型（Strings、Hashes、Lists、Sets、Sorted Sets）、高级类型（JSON、Geospatial、Bitmaps、Streams、Time Series、Vector Sets）、概率型数据结构（Bloom Filter、HyperLogLog等）\n- **主要应用场景**：缓存、分布式会话存储、NoSQL数据存储、数据结构服务器、搜索与查询引擎、事件存储与消息代理、生成式 AI向量存储、实时分析\n\n### 演示文稿摘要\n\nppt_generation生成的10页演示文稿《Redis: High-Performance Memory Data Store》包含以下幻灯片：\n1. **标题页**：Redis高性能内存数据存储\n2. **Redis是什么**：不仅是缓存，更是完整的数据结构服务器\n3. **核心架构与性能**：内存架构、亚毫秒延迟、单线程事件循环、RESP协议\n4. **丰富的数据结构**：基础类型、高级类型、概率型数据结构、专用类型\n5. **持久性与耐久性**：RDB快照、AOF日志、混合持久化\n6. **高可用与可扩展性**：主从复制、Sentinel故障转移、Cluster分片\n7. **主要应用场景：缓存与会话**：应用缓存、分布式会话、限流、实时分析\n8. **高级应用场景：搜索与消息**：Redis Search、Streams消息队列、Pub/Sub\n9. **AI与向量存储**：Vector Sets、语义搜索、RAG支持\n10. **总结与展望**：Redis在现代架构中的核心地位\n\n## # Web版式预览摘要frontend_design生成的 HTML版式草案包含以下区域：\n- **Hero区域**：深蓝色渐变背景配红色点缀，展示\"Redis —开源内存数据存储引擎\"标题\n- **项目概览卡片**：展示核心定位和六大关键特性（性能、灵活性、可扩展性、简洁性、普及性、多功能性）\n- **数据结构展示区**：卡片网格展示12+种数据结构\n- **应用场景区**：8个主要应用场景的卡片展示\n- **架构图说明**：分层架构的可视化展示\n- **关键文件证据表**：表格形式展示证据来源\n- **页脚**：GitHub和 redis.io链接\n\n## 十、总结\n\nRedis作为现代软件架构中不可或缺的基础组件，凭借其**内存级性能**、**丰富的数据结构**、**强大的持久化与集群能力**，已成为构建高性能应用的首选方案。无论是传统的缓存场景、实时消息系统，还是前沿的 AI向量存储应用，Redis都能提供稳定、高效、可扩展的解决方案。---<artifact language=\"html\" </div> </div> <div class=\"container\"> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>一、项目概览</h2> <p><strong>Redis</strong>（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的<strong>内存数据结构存储系统</strong>，被广泛用作数据库、缓存和消息代理。它支持丰富的数据结构，包括字符串（Strings）、哈希（Hashes）、列表（Lists）、集合（Sets）、有序集合（Sorted Sets）、位图（Bitmaps）、地理空间索引（Geospatial）、JSON文档、流（Streams）、时间序列（Time Series）以及向量集合（Vector Sets）等。</p> <p>Redis由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护，采用 BSD开源许可证。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>二、架构设计</h2> <h3>2.1整体架构分层</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">flowchart TD Client[\"客户端 (Client)\"] Net[\"网络层 (Networking)\"] RESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"] Cmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"] Data[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"] Mem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"] Pers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"] Client --&gt; Net Net --&gt; RESP RESP --&gt; Cmd Cmd --&gt; Data Data --&gt; Mem Mem --&gt; Pers classDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px; classDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b; classDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100; classDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20; class Client client; class Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core; class Mem,Pers storage;</code></pre> </div> <table> <thead> <tr><th>层级</th><th>职责</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>网络层</td><td>处理 TCP连接、多路复用（epoll/kqueue）、TLS加密通信</td></tr> <tr><td>协议层</td><td>实现 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol），高效解析客户端命令</td></tr> <tr><td>命令层</td><td>命令路由、权限检查、事务处理、Lua脚本执行</td></tr> <tr><td>数据模型层</td><td>实现各种数据结构（String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set、JSON、Stream等）</td></tr> <tr><td>内存存储层</td><td>管理内存分配、键空间、过期键淘汰策略</td></tr> <tr><td>持久化层</td><td>RDB快照和 AOF追加日志，确保数据不丢失</td></tr> </tbody> </table> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>三、架构图</h2> <p>Redis整体架构分层示意图（同上节2.1）。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>四、流程图</h2> <h3>4.1用户请求处理流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">sequenceDiagram participant Client participant RedisServer as Redis Server participant NetworkLayer as Network Layer participant RESPParser as RESP Parser participant CmdProcessor as Command Processor participant DataStructures as Data Structures participant MemoryStore as Memory Store participant Persistence as Persistence Layer Client-&gt;&gt;RedisServer: SEND command (RESP) RedisServer-&gt;&gt;NetworkLayer:接收 TCP连接 NetworkLayer-&gt;&gt;RESPParser:解析协议 RESPParser-&gt;&gt;CmdProcessor:执行命令 CmdProcessor-&gt;&gt;DataStructures:读写数据 DataStructures-&gt;&gt;MemoryStore:内存操作 MemoryStore-&gt;&gt;Persistence: RDB/AOF写入 MemoryStore-&gt;&gt;Client:返回结果</code></pre> </div> <h3>4.2 Agent执行流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">flowchart TD A[开始] --&gt; B[读取 GitHub仓库] B --&gt; C[读取文档/源码] C --&gt; D[web_fetch补充信息] D --&gt; E[生成架构图/流程图] E --&gt; F[生成项目介绍图片] F --&gt; G[生成中文长文稿件] G --&gt; H[生成演示文稿结构] H --&gt; I[生成 Web版式草案] I --&gt; J[输出最终 Markdown]</code></pre> </div> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>五、核心逻辑</h2> <h3>5.1单线程事件循环模型</h3> <p>Redis采用单线程事件循环模型处理客户端请求，带来原子性操作、低延迟和简化的并发模型。</p> <h3>5.2持久化机制</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>策略</th><th>说明</th><th>优点</th><th>缺点</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>RDB</td><td>定期创建数据快照</td><td>文件紧凑、恢复速度快</td><td>可能丢失最后一次快照后的数据</td></tr> <tr><td>AOF</td><td>记录每条写操作日志</td><td>数据安全性高</td><td>文件体积较大、恢复速度较慢</td></tr> </tbody> </table> <h3>5.3复制与分片</h3> <p>主从复制实现异步复制和读扩展；Redis Cluster实现自动数据分片和节点故障自动转移。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>六、重点特性</h2> <h3>6.1丰富的数据结构</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>类型</th><th>说明</th><th>适用场景</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>Strings</td><td>二进制安全的字符串</td><td>缓存、计数器、会话存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Hashes</td><td>字段-值对集合</td><td>用户信息存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Lists</td><td>双向链表</td><td>消息队列、时间线</td></tr> <tr><td>Sets</td><td>无序唯一集合</td><td>去重、共同好友</td></tr>\n <tr><td>Sorted Sets</td><td>带分数的有序集合</td><td>排行榜、延迟队列</td></tr> <tr><td>JSON</td><td>原生 JSON支持</td><td>文档存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Streams</td><td>追加日志式数据结构</td><td>事件溯源、消息队列</td></tr> <tr><td>Vector Sets</td><td>高维向量集合</td><td>AI 向量搜索、语义检索</td></tr>\n                    <tr><td>Bitmaps</td><td>位数组</td><td>用户活跃状态统计</td></tr> <tr><td>Geospatial</td><td>地理空间索引</td><td>位置服务、附近的人</td></tr> <tr><td>HyperLogLog</td><td>概率基数统计</td><td>独立访客计数</td></tr> <tr><td>Bloom Filter</td><td>布隆过滤器</td><td>缓存穿透防护</td></tr> </tbody> </table> <h3>6.2关键优势</h3> <ul> <li><strong>极致性能</strong>：亚毫秒级延迟，10万+ QPS</li> <li><strong>灵活性</strong>：支持多种数据结构和高级语义</li> <li><strong>可扩展性</strong>：Modules API允许扩展新命令</li> <li><strong>简洁性</strong>：RESP协议简单高效</li> <li><strong>普及性</strong>：被全球数百万应用广泛使用</li>\n            </ul>\n </section> <section class=\"section\"> class=\"img-container\"> <img src=\"https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/96b561e966ea48619ad7575fb67c9464.png\" alt=\"Redis架构介绍图\" width=\"1024\" height=\"1024\"> <p <p>newsletter_generation生成的中文长文《Redis开源内存数据存储引擎：架构、特性与应用场景深度解析》包含以下核心内容：</p> <ul> <li><strong>核心定位</strong>：Redis是首选的、速度最快且功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档及向量查询引擎</li> <li><strong>关键特性</strong>：极致性能（亚毫秒级延迟）、持久化机制（RDB/AOF）、高可用与扩展性（Sentinel/Cluster）、高级功能（Lua脚本、Modules API、RESP协议）</li> <li><strong>丰富的数据结构</strong>：基础类型、高级类型、概率型数据结构</li> <li><strong>主要应用场景</strong>：缓存、分布式会话存储、NoSQL数据存储、搜索与查询引擎、事件存储与消息代理、生成式 AI向量存储、实时分析</li> </ul> <h3>演示文稿摘要</h3> <p>ppt_generation生成的10页演示文稿《Redis: High-Performance Memory Data Store》包含以下幻灯片：</p> <ol> turn","summary":null,"startedAt":"2026-06-10T01:34:27.942649144Z","finishedAt":"2026-06-10T01:36:07.420855553Z","durationMs":"99478","errorCode":null,"message":"已达到工具调用限制。现在基于已有产物输出最终 Markdown。# Redis开源内存数据存储引擎：项目图文介绍\n\n## 一、项目概览\n\n**Redis**（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的**内存数据结构存储系统**，被广泛用作数据库、缓存和消息代理。它支持丰富的数据结构，包括字符串（Strings）、哈希（Hashes）、列表（Lists）、集合（Sets）、有序集合（Sorted Sets）、位图（Bitmaps）、地理空间索引（Geospatial）、JSON文档、流（Streams）、时间序列（Time Series）以及向量集合（Vector Sets）等。Redis的核心定位是：\n- **首选的、速度最快且功能最丰富的缓存服务器**\n- **数据结构服务器**：提供底层数据结构和高级语义（计数器、队列、排行榜、限流器等）\n- **文档和向量查询引擎**：通过 Redis Search支持全文搜索、向量搜索、地理空间查询\n- **事件存储和消息代理**：支持 Streams、Pub/Sub、优先级队列等Redis由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护，采用 BSD开源许可证。\n\n## 二、架构设计\n\n### 2.1整体架构分层Redis的架构采用分层设计，从网络通信到数据存储逐层抽象：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"]\nNet[\"网络层 (Networking)\"]\nRESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"]\nCmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"]\nData[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"]\nMem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"]\nPers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"]\nClient --> Net\nNet --> RESP\nRESP --> Cmd\nCmd --> Data\nData --> Mem\nMem --> Pers\nclassDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;\nclassDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b;\nclassDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100;\nclassDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20;\nclass Client client;\nclass Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core;\nclass Mem,Pers storage;\n```\n\n**各层职责说明：**|层级 |职责 |\n|------|------|\n| **网络层** |处理 TCP连接、多路复用（epoll/kqueue）、TLS加密通信 |\n| **协议层** |实现 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol），高效解析客户端命令 |\n| **命令层** |命令路由、权限检查、事务处理、Lua脚本执行 |\n| **数据模型层** |实现各种数据结构（String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set、JSON、Stream等） |\n| **内存存储层** |管理内存分配、键空间、过期键淘汰策略 |\n| **持久化层** | RDB快照和 AOF追加日志，确保数据不丢失 |\n\n### 2.2高可用架构Redis提供两种高可用方案：\n- **Redis Sentinel**：监控主从节点，自动故障转移，保证服务可用性\n- **Redis Cluster**：原生分片支持，自动数据分区，实现水平扩展\n\n## 三、架构图\n\n以下是 Redis整体架构的分层示意图：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"]\nNet[\"网络层 (Networking)\"]\nRESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"]\nCmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"]\nData[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"]\nMem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"]\nPers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"]\nClient --> Net\nNet --> RESP\nRESP --> Cmd\nCmd --> Data\nData --> Mem\nMem --> Pers\nclassDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;\nclassDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b;\nclassDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100;\nclassDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20;\nclass Client client;\nclass Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core;\nclass Mem,Pers storage;\n```\n\n## 四、流程图\n\n### 4.1用户请求处理流程\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\nparticipant Client\nparticipant RedisServer as Redis Server\nparticipant NetworkLayer as Network Layer\nparticipant RESPParser as RESP Parser\nparticipant CmdProcessor as Command Processor\nparticipant DataStructures as Data Structures\nparticipant MemoryStore as Memory Store\nparticipant Persistence as Persistence Layer\nClient->>RedisServer: SEND command (RESP)\nRedisServer->>NetworkLayer:接收 TCP连接\nNetworkLayer->>RESPParser:解析协议\nRESPParser->>CmdProcessor:执行命令\nCmdProcessor->>DataStructures:读写数据\nDataStructures->>MemoryStore:内存操作\nMemoryStore->>Persistence: RDB/AOF写入\nMemoryStore->>Client:返回结果\n```\n\n### 4.2 Agent执行流程\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nA[开始] --> B[读取 GitHub仓库]\nB --> C[读取文档/源码]\nC --> D[web_fetch补充信息]\nD --> E[生成架构图/流程图]\nE --> F[生成项目介绍图片]\nF --> G[生成中文长文稿件]\nG --> H[生成演示文稿结构]\nH --> I[生成 Web版式草案]\nI --> J[输出最终 Markdown]\n```\n\n## 五、核心逻辑\n\n### 5.1单线程事件循环\n\n模型\n\nRedis 采用单线程事件循环模型处理客户端请求，这带来了以下优势：\n- **原子性操作**：所有命令按顺序执行，无需锁机制\n- **低延迟**：避免上下文切换和锁竞争\n- **简化并发模型**：开发者无需担心竞态条件\n\n### 5.2持久化机制Redis提供两种持久化策略：\n\n|策略 |说明 |优点 |缺点 |\n|------|------|------|------|\n| **RDB** |定期创建数据快照 |文件紧凑、恢复速度快 |可能丢失最后一次快照后的数据 |\n| **AOF** |记录每条写操作日志 |数据安全性高 |文件体积较大、恢复速度较慢 |Redis4.0+支持混合持久化，结合 RDB和 AOF的优点。\n\n### 5.3复制与分片\n- **主从复制**：异步复制，从节点可用于读扩展和故障转移\n- **Redis Cluster**：自动数据分片（16384个哈希槽），支持节点故障自动转移\n\n## 六、重点特性\n\n### 6.1丰富的数据结构\n\nRedis支持15+种原生数据结构：|类型 |说明 |适用场景 |\n|------|------|----------|\n| **Strings** |二进制安全的字符串 |缓存、计数器、会话存储 |\n| **Hashes** |字段-值对集合 |用户信息存储 |\n| **Lists** |双向链表 |消息队列、时间线 |\n| **Sets** |无序唯一集合 |去重、共同好友 |\n| **Sorted Sets** |带分数的有序集合 |排行榜、延迟队列 |\n| **JSON** |原生 JSON支持 |文档存储 |\n| **Streams** |追加日志式数据结构 |事件溯源、消息队列 |\n| **Vector Sets** |高维向量集合 | AI向量搜索、语义检索 |\n| **Bitmaps** |位数组 |用户活跃状态统计 |\n| **Geospatial** |地理空间索引 |位置服务、附近的人 |\n| **HyperLogLog** |概率基数统计 |独立访客计数 |\n| **Bloom Filter** |布隆过滤器 |缓存穿透防护 |\n\n### 6.2关键优势\n- **极致性能**：亚毫秒级延迟，10万+ QPS\n- **灵活性**：支持多种数据结构和高级语义\n- **可扩展性**：Modules API允许扩展新命令\n- **简洁性**：RESP协议简单高效\n- **普及性**：被全球数百万应用广泛使用\n\n## 七、关键文件证据表\n\n|文件路径 |用途 |证据说明 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| `README.md` |项目概述、核心特性、使用场景 |确认 Redis的定位、数据结构、持久化机制、高可用方案 |\n| `CONTRIBUTING.md` |代码贡献指南 |确认项目开源社区规范 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/` |开发文档 |确认客户端 API、数据操作方式 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/` |数据类型文档 |确认15+种数据结构的详细说明 |\n\n## </div> </div> <div class=\"container\"> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>一、项目概览</h2> <p><strong>Redis</strong>（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的<strong>内存数据结构存储系统</strong>，被广泛用作数据库、缓存和消息代理。它支持丰富的数据结构，包括字符串（Strings）、哈希（Hashes）、列表（Lists）、集合（Sets）、有序集合（Sorted Sets）、位图（Bitmaps）、地理空间索引（Geospatial）、JSON文档、流（Streams）、时间序列（Time Series）以及向量集合（Vector Sets）等。</p> <p>Redis由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护，采用 BSD开源许可证。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>二、架构设计</h2> <h3>2.1整体架构分层</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">flowchart TD Client[\"客户端 (Client)\"] Net[\"网络层 (Networking)\"] RESP[\"Redis协议层 (RESP)\"] Cmd[\"命令处理层 (Command)\"] Data[\"数据模型层 (Data Structures)\"] Mem[\"内存存储层 (Memory Store)\"] Pers[\"持久化层 (RDB/AOF)\"] Client --&gt; Net Net --&gt; RESP RESP --&gt; Cmd Cmd --&gt; Data Data --&gt; Mem Mem --&gt; Pers classDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px; classDef client fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b; classDef core fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100; classDef storage fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#1b5e20; class Client client; class Net,RESP,Cmd,Data,Mem,Pers core; class Mem,Pers storage;</code></pre> </div> <table> <thead> <tr><th>层级</th><th>职责</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>网络层</td><td>处理 TCP连接、多路复用（epoll/kqueue）、TLS加密通信</td></tr> <tr><td>协议层</td><td>实现 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol），高效解析客户端命令</td></tr> <tr><td>命令层</td><td>命令路由、权限检查、事务处理、Lua脚本执行</td></tr> <tr><td>数据模型层</td><td>实现各种数据结构（String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set、JSON、Stream等）</td></tr> <tr><td>内存存储层</td><td>管理内存分配、键空间、过期键淘汰策略</td></tr> <tr><td>持久化层</td><td>RDB快照和 AOF追加日志，确保数据不丢失</td></tr> </tbody> </table> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>三、架构图</h2> <p>Redis整体架构分层示意图（同上节2.1）。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>四、流程图</h2> <h3>4.1用户请求处理流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">sequenceDiagram participant Client participant RedisServer as Redis Server participant NetworkLayer as Network Layer participant RESPParser as RESP Parser participant CmdProcessor as Command Processor participant DataStructures as Data Structures participant MemoryStore as Memory Store participant Persistence as Persistence Layer Client-&gt;&gt;RedisServer: SEND command (RESP) RedisServer-&gt;&gt;NetworkLayer:接收 TCP连接 NetworkLayer-&gt;&gt;RESPParser:解析协议 RESPParser-&gt;&gt;CmdProcessor:执行命令 CmdProcessor-&gt;&gt;DataStructures:读写数据 DataStructures-&gt;&gt;MemoryStore:内存操作 MemoryStore-&gt;&gt;Persistence: RDB/AOF写入 MemoryStore-&gt;&gt;Client:返回结果</code></pre> </div> <h3>4.2 Agent执行流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-container\"> <pre><code class=\"language-mermaid\">flowchart TD A[开始] --&gt; B[读取 GitHub仓库] B --&gt; C[读取文档/源码] C --&gt; D[web_fetch补充信息] D --&gt; E[生成架构图/流程图] E --&gt; F[生成项目介绍图片] F --&gt; G[生成中文长文稿件] G --&gt; H[生成演示文稿结构] H --&gt; I[生成 Web版式草案] I --&gt; J[输出最终 Markdown]</code></pre> </div> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>五、核心逻辑</h2> <h3>5.1单线程事件循环模型</h3> <p>Redis采用单线程事件循环模型处理客户端请求，带来原子性操作、低延迟和简化的并发模型。</p> <h3>5.2持久化机制</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>策略</th><th>说明</th><th>优点</th><th>缺点</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>RDB</td><td>定期创建数据快照</td><td>文件紧凑、恢复速度快</td><td>可能丢失最后一次快照后的数据</td></tr> <tr><td>AOF</td><td>记录每条写操作日志</td><td>数据安全性高</td><td>文件体积较大、恢复速度较慢</td></tr> </tbody> </table> <h3>5.3复制与分片</h3> <p>主从复制实现异步复制和读扩展；Redis Cluster实现自动数据分片和节点故障自动转移。</p> </section> <section class=\"section\"> <h2>六、重点特性</h2> <h3>6.1丰富的数据结构</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>类型</th><th>说明</th><th>适用场景</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>Strings</td><td>二进制安全的字符串</td><td>缓存、计数器、会话存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Hashes</td><td>字段-值对集合</td><td>用户信息存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Lists</td><td>双向链表</td><td>消息队列、时间线</td></tr> <tr><td>Sets</td><td>无序唯一集合</td><td>去重、共同好友</td></tr>\n <tr><td>Sorted Sets</td><td>带分数的有序集合</td><td>排行榜、延迟队列</td></tr> <tr><td>JSON</td><td>原生 JSON支持</td><td>文档存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Streams</td><td>追加日志式数据结构</td><td>事件溯源、消息队列</td></tr> <tr><td>Vector Sets</td><td>高维向量集合</td><td>AI 向量搜索、语义检索</td></tr>\n                    <tr><td>Bitmaps</td><td>位数组</td><td>用户活跃状态统计</td></tr> <tr><td>Geospatial</td><td>地理空间索引</td><td>位置服务、附近的人</td></tr> <tr><td>HyperLogLog</td><td>概率基数统计</td><td>独立访客计数</td></tr> <tr><td>Bloom Filter</td><td>布隆过滤器</td><td>缓存穿透防护</td></tr> </tbody> </table> <h3>6.2关键优势</h3> <ul> <li><strong>极致性能</strong>：亚毫秒级延迟，10万+ QPS</li> <li><strong>灵活性</strong>：支持多种数据结构和高级语义</li> <li><strong>可扩展性</strong>：Modules API允许扩展新命令</li> <li><strong>简洁性</strong>：RESP协议简单高效</li> <li><strong>普及性</strong>：被全球数百万应用广泛使用</li>\n            </ul>\n </section> <section class=\"section\">
